compiled_state.title: As a Java developer, project-detect.sh recognizes mvn test and ./gradlew test in GitHub Actions YAML
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add pattern recognition to project-detect.sh so it identifies 'mvn test' and './gradlew test' (and 'gradle test') as Java CI test patterns when scanning GitHub Actions YAML.

**Why**: project-detect.sh today cannot identify Java CI pipelines. Java projects' CI YAML is reported as unknown stack, preventing accurate project detection and roadmap routing.

**Scope**:
- IN: project-detect.sh: add mvn test, ./gradlew test, gradle test patterns to the GitHub Actions YAML scanner; treat java-maven and java-gradle as equivalent in detection (per equivalence contract); report detected stack as 'java' (or 'java-maven'/'java-gradle' based on which pattern matches)
- OUT: detect-stack.sh (Story F1); onboarding defaults (Story F2); gate extensions (Story F3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, project-detect.sh correctly identifies a GitHub Actions YAML containing 'mvn test' as a Java/Maven project
  ← Satisfies: "project-detect.sh recognizes mvn test and ./gradlew test patterns in GitHub Actions YAML"
- When this story is complete, project-detect.sh correctly identifies a YAML containing './gradlew test' as a Java/Gradle project
  ← Satisfies: "project-detect.sh recognizes mvn test and ./gradlew test patterns"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for both mvn and gradlew pattern recognition

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Story F4 depends on Story F1 (equivalence contract for how to handle java-maven vs java-gradle in detection)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
